---
title: "Signpost Monitoring — Marseille TFPB"
output: html_document
---

## Decision-Testing Method: Monte Carlo Simulation

We simulate 10,000 draws of annual TFPB revenue over 3 years (2025–2027) 
under three strategies, allowing key parameters to vary stochastically.

```{r monte-carlo, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(here)
library(gt)


set.seed(42)
n_sim <- 10000

# --- Base parameters (from REI 2024) ---
base_2024      <- 1128490752   # base nette (€)
taux_2024      <- 0.4454       # taux municipal voté
revenue_2024   <- 502900789    # montant réel (€)
n_articles     <- 349502       # nombre d'articles
vacants_struct <- 14529        # vacants structurels (LOVAC 2024)
base_per_unit  <- 3229         # base cadastrale par article (€)

# --- Uncertain parameters (distributions) ---
# Base growth rate: mean 3.5%, sd 2% (reflects cadastral revalorisation uncertainty)
base_growth  <- matrix(rnorm(n_sim * 3, mean = 0.035, sd = 0.02), 
                        nrow = n_sim, ncol = 3)

# Vacancy mobilisation rate: mean 25%, sd 10%
mobil_rate   <- matrix(pmax(0, rnorm(n_sim * 3, mean = 0.25, sd = 0.10)), 
                        nrow = n_sim, ncol = 3)

# Rate elasticity shock: small random shock on effective rate collection
rate_shock   <- matrix(rnorm(n_sim * 3, mean = 0, sd = 0.005), 
                        nrow = n_sim, ncol = 3)

# --- Revenue simulation function ---
simulate_revenue <- function(strategy) {
  revenues <- matrix(0, nrow = n_sim, ncol = 3)
  base_t <- base_2024
  
  for (t in 1:3) {
    # Base evolves with stochastic growth
    base_t <- base_t * (1 + base_growth[, t])
    
    if (strategy == "rate_hike") {
      # +2pp rate increase on municipal portion only
      taux_t <- taux_2024 + 0.02 + rate_shock[, t]
      revenues[, t] <- base_t * taux_t
      
    } else if (strategy == "vacancy") {
      # Vacancy mobilisation: new articles enter the base
      new_articles <- vacants_struct * mobil_rate[, t]
      extra_base   <- new_articles * base_per_unit
      revenues[, t] <- (base_t + extra_base) * (taux_2024 + rate_shock[, t])
      
    } else if (strategy == "mixed") {
      # +1pp rate + 25% vacancy mobilisation
      taux_t       <- taux_2024 + 0.01 + rate_shock[, t]
      new_articles <- vacants_struct * mobil_rate[, t] * 0.5
      extra_base   <- new_articles * base_per_unit
      revenues[, t] <- (base_t + extra_base) * taux_t
    }
  }
  return(revenues)
}

# --- Run simulations ---
rev_rate    <- simulate_revenue("rate_hike")
rev_vacancy <- simulate_revenue("vacancy")
rev_mixed   <- simulate_revenue("mixed")

# --- Extract year 3 distributions (M€) ---
results <- data.frame(
  Rate_hike    = rev_rate[, 3] / 1e6,
  Vacancy      = rev_vacancy[, 3] / 1e6,
  Mixed        = rev_mixed[, 3] / 1e6
) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Strategy", values_to = "Revenue_M")

# --- Plot distributions ---
plot <- ggplot(results, aes(x = Revenue_M, fill = Strategy)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = revenue_2024 / 1e6, 
             linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Rate_hike" = "#e74c3c", 
                                "Vacancy"   = "#2980b9", 
                                "Mixed"     = "#8e44ad"),
                    labels = c("Rate +2pp", "Vacancy mobilisation", "Mixed")) +
  labs(title = "Monte Carlo simulation — TFPB revenue distribution (Year 3, 2027)",
       subtitle = "10,000 simulations | Dashed line = 2024 actual revenue",
       x = "Projected revenue (M€)", y = "Density", fill = "Strategy") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
ggsave(here("output", "Monte_Carlo_Simulation.png"))
plot
```
```{r}
summary_stats <- results %>%
  group_by(Strategy) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean   = round(mean(Revenue_M), 1),
    Median = round(median(Revenue_M), 1),
    P10    = round(quantile(Revenue_M, 0.10), 1),
    P90    = round(quantile(Revenue_M, 0.90), 1),
    SD     = round(sd(Revenue_M), 1),
    `P(>2024)` = round(mean(Revenue_M > revenue_2024 / 1e6) * 100, 1)
  )

tab <- summary_stats %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    Strategy = "Strategy",
    Mean = "Mean (M€)",
    Median = "Median (M€)",
    P10 = "P10 (M€)",
    P90 = "P90 (M€)",
    SD = "SD (M€)",
    `P(>2024)` = "P(beat 2024, %)"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Monte Carlo summary statistics",
    subtitle = "Year 3 (2027)"
  )
gtsave(tab, here("output","Monte_Carlo_Statistics.png"))
tab
```
